# orcasecurity_custom_compliance_framework (Resource)

Provides a custom compliance framework resource. Use this resource to create and manage custom compliance frameworks in Orca Security.

~> **Note:** Sections and their tests are **write-only**. The API does not return section/test data on read, so Terraform cannot detect drift for sections modified outside of Terraform. Terraform preserves the last-applied value in state.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "orcasecurity_custom_compliance_framework" "example" {
  name        = "My Custom Framework"
  description = "Custom compliance framework managed by Terraform"

  sections = [
    {
      name = "Access Control"
      tests = [
        {
          rule_id              = "your_rule_id"
          rule_id_in_framework = "1.1"
        },
        {
          rule_id              = "your_rule_id"
          rule_id_in_framework = "1.2"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      name = "Data Protection"
      tests = [
        {
          rule_id              = "your_rule_id"
          rule_id_in_framework = "2.1"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Framework name.
- `description` (String) Framework description.
- `sections` (Attributes List) Framework sections containing tests/controls. Sections are write-only and not returned by the API on read. Terraform will preserve the last-applied value in state. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sections))

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Framework ID.

<a id="nestedatt--sections"></a>

### Nested Schema for `sections`

Required:

- `name` (String) Section name.
- `tests` (Attributes List) Tests (controls) within this section. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--sections--tests))

<a id="nestedatt--sections--tests"></a>

### Nested Schema for `sections.tests`

Required:

- `rule_id` (String) The rule ID for the test/control.
- `rule_id_in_framework` (String) The identifier for this rule within the framework. Use dotted notation (e.g., `1.1`, `1.2`) for proper section grouping in the Orca UI.

## Import

Custom compliance frameworks can be imported using the framework ID:

```shell
terraform import orcasecurity_custom_compliance_framework.example 2815
```

~> **Note:** After import, only `id`, `name`, and `description` are populated. The `sections` attribute will be empty because the API does not return section data. You must add sections to your Terraform configuration and run `terraform apply` to sync state.
